title: Lines, Containers, Headings, Paragraphs, Lists, Quotes, and Fenced Code · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent documentation team receives a different content tree, language, route base, template, asset set, trust policy, hosting target, and accessibility need and transfers lines, containers, headings, paragraphs, lists, quotes, and fenced code into a changed line-oriented block parser with explicit continuation rules. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of line-oriented block parser with explicit continuation rules with automated and judgment evidence. Learne…
objectives: Represent line content, indentation, blankness, offset, tab expansion, newline, and container prefix without discarding source positions.
Apply named block-start and continuation precedence so paragraphs, headings, quotes, lists, thematic breaks, and code resolve consistently.
Parse ATX headings and paragraphs with closing-marker, interruption, inline-content, and empty-heading evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Line Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab publishing team handles case 4718 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to parse headings, paragraphs, containers, and fenced code with explicit line consumption; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Line Model.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-fenced-code-1rmzto
# Competency: ssg-fenced-code.
# Case 9916: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_fenced_code_1rmzto(lines: int = 28, consumed_lines: int = 28, blocks: int = 9, invalid_containers: int = 0, unclosed_fences: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1rmzto = "1rmzto-otzmr1-9916"
    assert evidence_variant_1rmzto
    evidence_axes_1rmzto = (
        "reader1rmzto",
        "source9916",
        "parseotzmr1",
        "route1rmzto9916",
        "artifact99161rmzto",
        "recoveryotzmr19916",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1rmzto)) == 6
    if lines < 1 or consumed_lines < 0:
        return False, consumed_lines, "line-count"
    if consumed_lines != lines:
        return False, consumed_lines, "line-consumption"
    if blocks < 1 or blocks > lines:
        return False, blocks, "block-shape"
    if invalid_containers > 0 or unclosed_fences > 0:
        return False, invalid_containers + unclosed_fences, "block-boundary"
    assert consumed_lines == lines
    return True, blocks, "bounded-block-tree"
